prevalence.source: Brazilian newborn screening (triagem neonatal) and hematology program syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable births; improved survival increases prevalent population

incidence.source: National newborn screening program reports (PNTN) and hematology networks

burden.dalyPerCapita: low nationally; high per-patient burden (pain crises, organ damage)
burden.mortalityRate: Elevated vs general population; reduced with early care, vaccination and hydroxyurea
burden.source: IHME/GBD hemoglobinopathies context; clinical cohort outcomes

riskFactors: limited access to early prophylaxis and comprehensive care
vulnerablePopulations: patients with poor access to hematology services
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Newborn screening or hemoglobin electrophoresis/HPLC; confirm genotype; monitor complications (stroke risk, pulmonary hypertension, renal disease)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: newborn screening widely implemented; follow-up varies by region
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Tens of thousands living with SCD
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with comprehensive care, prophylaxis and hydroxyurea access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: PNTN newborn screening with confirmatory testing; referral to hematology services for longitudinal care
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Penicillin prophylaxis in childhood, vaccination, hydroxyurea when indicated, transfusion protocols for acute complications, pain management, stroke prevention and TCD screening where available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Care fragmentation, limited specialized centers in some states, under-resourced pain management and transition to adult care
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established newborn screening pathway and national clinical experience; hydroxyurea widely used in public hematology programs
